description: Kalshi market calibration bias from 7.3M resolved markets. Positive gap = market underprices YES (buy YES). Negative gap = market overprices YES (buy NO).
key_insight: Kalshi markets in the 20-50 cent range systematically overstate YES probability by 6-10%. The biggest edge is buying NO on markets priced 40-50 cents (actual YES rate only 35% vs implied 45%).
strategy_implication: When the ensemble says NO on a market priced 30-50c, the historical bias supports that trade. When it says YES on a market priced 70-90c, history also supports it.
